customer_anxiety_profile: Stephanie is not hostile but is time-constrained (night-shift worker, weekends only). Her primary friction was conceptual confusion about payment structure, which resolved by mid-call. She is self-persuaded on the value logic but has not seen her actual numbers. She is not a skeptic — she is a busy person who needs the appointment to be low-friction.
unresolved_concerns: Payment structure confusion partially resolved but never cleanly closed — closer should open with a crisp one-sentence restatement of bill replacement
Bill not captured — appointment may not be sit-eligible if this is a paid lead; bill photo is pending via Parker's text follow-up
No 'at your computer' or 'one hour block' expectation set — closer should re-anchor these at the start of the call
sensitive_topics_raised: Financial constraint mentioned at outset ('I don't have any money right now') — closer should not lead with cost; lead with savings and bill replacement
Night-shift schedule — do not call before early afternoon on Saturday; 2pm was the agreed time
exact_promises_rep_made: Text sent to 773 number for bill photo follow-up
Specialist will go over warranties and installation details before anything is official
closer_should_know: Stephanie self-articulated the value proposition at 01:36 — she understands the bill-replacement logic and is open to seeing her numbers. The appointment is warm but structurally fragile: no bill on file, no button-up protocol completed, and she is a night-shift worker who will not answer early calls. Confirm the bill came through before the Saturday call. If it did not, the closer is walking in blind on system sizing.
